title: DRILL 2.5 Computing the Zero-State Response
summary: This page concludes a convolution example for continuous-time systems, showing graphically how x(t) and h(t) produce y(t) = (e^{-t} - e^{-2t})u(t). It also introduces Drill 2.5, asking for zero-state responses of an LTIC system to two inputs and providing the answers.

figures.caption: Convolution of x(t) and h(t), showing plots (a) x(t)=e^-t, (b) h(t)=e^-2t, and (c) y(t)
